01What I do

A marketer who can ship the product too.

Since 2020 I've run paid acquisition for e-commerce, where a campaign either turns a profit or gets switched off. When it needs a funnel, a site, or an app, I build it.

Growth side

Run the campaigns

  • Meta & Google paid acquisition, end to end
  • Pixel & Conversions API, CBO, Advantage+
  • Conversion rate & funnel optimisation
  • Built around cost per order and margin
Build side

Build what it points to

  • Web & mobile apps (React Native, Next.js)
  • WordPress & WooCommerce stores
  • Backends & data (Supabase, PostGIS)
  • AI image & video creative pipelines
02Featured · try it

Àjálà, running right here.

Tap the buttons, switch Danfo / BRT / Ferry, or swipe the phone. It's the real app, embedded. I designed and built the app, the website, and the routing engine.

Àjálà · Lagos transit & safety co-pilot

A co-pilot for Lagos's informal transport. It finds Danfo, BRT and Ferry routes, tells you the exact conductor shout to listen for, crowdsources live road intel, and shares your trip with a trusted contact. A tab only shows when that mode exists for your trip.

React NativeNext.jsSupabasePostGISVercel
Visit ajala.live →
Heads up: the three route tabs show three real example trips from the app (Danfo, BRT and Ferry routes were captured on different journeys).
ajala.live

04My brand · growth

Spotshop NG, I design the funnel and run the ads.

My own e-commerce brand. I design the product funnels and run the paid acquisition behind them: 100+ Meta & Google campaigns, 1,000+ orders for one product in three months, cost per order kept well below margin.

Swipe or use the arrows. These are live product funnels I designed.

Product funnels that convert

Each product gets a focused landing page built to turn an ad click into an order: clear hero, price, proof, and a one-tap order line. I run the design and the paid traffic end to end.

Meta AdsGoogle AdsLanding pagesCROWhatsApp commerce
The full Spotshop storefront (home accessories, appliances, utility tools) is the design we tackle next.
